title: Cannot limit memory when started by systemd as a user
body:
/kind bug
**Description**

Problem: ```podman[6826]: Error: cannot set limits without cgroups: OCI runtime error```

I have systemd service under /etc/systemd which is to start container as a user. It fails like above ^

Opening a new issues for this case https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7765 where I came late at comment https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7765#issuecomment-703300218


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. create service

```
cat >>/etc/systemd/system/test.service<<EOF
[Unit]
Description=Podman Container
After=network.target

[Service]
Type=simple
TimeoutStartSec=15
ExecStartPre=-/usr/bin/rm -f /%T/%n-pid /%T/%n-cid
User=pilivifi

ExecStart=/usr/bin/podman run --name pilivi \
  --rm -p 8091:80/tcp  --memory="128M" \
  --conmon-pidfile  /%T/%n-pid --cidfile /%T/%n-cid \
  sebp/lighttpd 
ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c "/usr/bin/podman stop -t "15" `cat /%T/%n-cid`"
# ExecStop=/usr/bin/sh -c "/usr/bin/podman rm -f `cat /%T/%n-cid`"
Restart=on-failure
RestartSec=30
KillMode=none
PIDFile=/%T/%n-pid

[Install]
WantedBy=multi-user.target
EOF
```
2. refresh systemd ```systemctl daemon-reload```

3. create user pilivifi

3. do ```systemctl start test.service```

**Describe the results you received:**

You need to login as pilivifi user, not by ```sudo su - pilivifi```, but really login using ```su -```

You'll see from user's journalctl that container failed with:
```podman[6826]: Error: cannot set limits without cgroups: OCI runtime error```


**Describe the results you expected:**

container to start and be seen under user pilivifi


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

always

As mentioned in the previous ticket, I tried the tips there, but they didn't help:

```
[Service]
Delegate=yes
```

```
cat /etc/systemd/system/user@.service.d
[Service]
Delegate=cpu io memory pids
```

cgroups v2 is available:
```
grep cgroup /proc/filesystems
nodev   cgroup
nodev   cgroup2
```


**Output of `podman version`:**

system is up to date fedora-iot

```
Version:      2.0.6
API Version:  1
Go Version:   go1.14.6
Built:        Tue Sep  1 22:26:51 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

No

comments.body: A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

This might be a case where you need to enable linger?
@giuseppe Correct?

yes we need that, but in general we don't support running rootless containers with `User=` in a system service file.  We will still need to make sure the user session starts before the service.

I suggest to enable linger mode and run the service as part of the user session (installed under `~/.config/systemd/user/`)

Anyone ending up here while wondering how to run rootless containers with ansible, this commit has the changes easily visible. It's lacking from ansible docs at the moment https://github.com/ikke-t/podman-container-systemd/commit/3d03115a0df0d2e1bfae9cb854a8f0688b655733
